page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the n1 server family. It is configured with 8 vCPUs and 30 GB of RAM, translating to 3.75 GB of memory per core. The instance runs on the x86_64 architecture and is powered by an Intel Xeon processor operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The underlying hardware features 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation. The processor includes a 45 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, and BMI2 instruction sets. This server configuration does not include any local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the instance sits primarily in the lower performance tiers for compute-heavy tasks. Both single-core and multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng fall into the bottom 25% tier, as do the Geekbench multi-core and software compilation benchmarks. Single-threaded compression and decompression tests register in the bottom 10%. However, the server demonstrates average performance in large-block memory bandwidth tests, both for read and write operations, and achieves middle-tier results in Geekbench ray tracing and small-model LLM text generation.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, this instance offers a balanced resource density suitable for workloads that do not demand high-performance computing. It is best utilized for general-purpose applications, including static web serving, moderate database operations, and basic background utility tasks. The lack of local storage and GPU acceleration makes it less suitable for data-intensive storage systems or machine learning training workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the n1 family, configured with 8 vCPUs and 30 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.3 GHz, it features 4 physical cores with hyper-threading and dedicated CPU allocation. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data indicates weak single-core and multi-core CPU performance, placing it in the bottom 25% tier, though large-block memory bandwidth and ray tracing benchmarks reach average levels. Given its balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 3.75 GB per core, this instance is suited for moderate general-purpose workloads, including basic web serving, small database operations, and lightweight background tasks where high-performance computing is not required.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 8 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, offering balanced resources for moderate workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is a general-purpose server featuring 8 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced virtual machine featuring dedicated processors and moderate memory capacity, designed for standard workloads and general-purpose applications without high compute demands.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 30 GB of RAM on x86_64 architecture
Dedicated Intel Xeon CPU running at 2.3 GHz with 4 physical cores
Average performance in large-block memory bandwidth and ray tracing
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for basic web serving, small databases, and general-purpose tasks
